latest.title: AGM Statement and Business Update
latest.content:
3 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5405G
Imaging Biometrics Limited
03 June 2026
IMAGING BIOMETRICS LIMITED
("IBAI" or the "Company")
(LSE: IBAI)
AGM Statement and Business Update
3 June 2026
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) is pleased to provide this update to shareholders
ahead of
its Annual General Meeting
being
held today. The Group develops quantitative neuroimaging software and organ-on-a-chip technology for healthcare and life sciences.
IB Nimble™ 2 - Commercial Launch
The Company today announces the release of IB Nimble™ 2, a substantially upgraded version of its secure mobile and web-based platform for multidisciplinary clinical collaboration. It is available on web, iOS, and Android. It is designed to support tumour board discussions, complex disease management workflows, and emergent care settings.
The release incorporates material enhancements developed in direct response to feedback from existing clinical users. In-app DICOM (medical image format) viewing allows care teams to review imaging within the platform without switching between systems. PACS (picture archiving and communication system) synchronisation makes imaging data accessible in clinical context without local installation. The release also adds customisable roles and permissions, synchronised cross-platform experience, and reinforced cybersecurity architecture.
IB Nimble™ 2 is available immediately to both existing and prospective customers. The Board expects the release to broaden the platform's addressability to institutions not previously able to justify adoption without integrated imaging capability.
IB Nimble™ 2 forms part of the Company's broader strategy to sharpen its focus on its neuroimaging software portfolio, prioritising high-quality, commercially sustainable products, as outlined in the strategic review published in February 2026.
"The ability to sync with PACS and view DICOM images within IB Nimble™ 2 fundamentally changes how clinicians and researchers collaborate. Users can now review imaging data in context, alongside colleagues, in real time - without switching between systems. That is a meaningful improvement in both efficiency and clinical decision-making."
- Dr Joseph Bovi MD, Physician Chief, ThedaCare Cancer Service Line
"IB Nimble™ 2 is the direct product of listening to the clinicians who use our platform every day. Integrated image viewing within a mobile collaboration tool removes a genuine friction point in tumour board workflows. We are pleased to deliver this to our user base and to make it available to new institutions as we continue to build on the commercial momentum established in 2025."
- Trevor Brown, Chief Executive, Imaging Biometrics Limited
QSMetric™ - GE HealthCare Distribution
In December 2025, the Company's distribution relationship with GE HealthCare was expanded. The expanded agreement now covers QSMetric™ and FTB Express Maps alongside the existing IB Neuro® and IB Delta T1™ products. This gives IBAI's software access to GE's installed base of MRI systems worldwide.
QSMetric™ is a patented, FDA-cleared quantitative susceptibility mapping (QSM) solution developed and validated at Weill Cornell Medicine. QSMetric™ detects and measures susceptibility-based biomarkers-particularly iron and microhemorrhages-supporting diagnosis, monitoring, and treatment planning across a range of neurological conditions including amyloid-related imaging abnormalities - hemorrhagic (ARIA-H) in patients receiving anti-amyloid Alzheimer's therapies. FDA-mandated ARIA monitoring is a protocol requirement for these treatments.
IBAI contributes its certified Quality Management System, regulatory expertise, and global support infrastructure. Integration of QSMetric™ into GE's distribution ecosystem is progressing, and the Board views this as an incremental revenue opportunity for the Group.
Kirkstall Limited - Organ-on-a-Chip Platform
Kirkstall, acquired in October 2025, delivered year-on-year revenue growth in 2025 and has a distributor network spanning the United States, China, and South Korea. New distribution partners have been added in recent months, further expanding commercial reach.
The Quasi-Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform is in active use in pharmaceutical research, toxicology, and disease modelling. A BBSRC/NC3Rs-funded collaboration with Nottingham Trent University is developing a glioblastoma tumour-on-a-chip model. This opens a route into pharmaceutical drug discovery, a segment with larger and recurring order volumes.
Kirkstall is transitioning from a university-centric business toward a commercially scalable operation. The platform is well positioned to benefit from the global regulatory shift away from animal testing, including the US FDA Modernization Act 2.0 and equivalent mandates in the UK, EU, China and South Korea.
Gallium Maltolate - Phase 1 Publication
Following the successful completion of the Phase 1 clinical trial of oral gallium maltolate (GaM) in November 2025, preparation of the study results for journal submission is continuing. The Board expects submission in 2026 and will update shareholders once it has been made. The Group retains contractual rights to the Phase 1 data for regulatory and commercialisation purposes.
Chief Executive's Statement
"Imaging Biometrics enters this AGM with commercial momentum that is genuine and measurable. Our neuroimaging software is now distributed by global partners. Our mobile collaboration platform has been substantially upgraded. Our organ-on-a-chip business is generating early commercial traction. With IB Nimble™ 2 launched and the enhanced IB Clinic platform nearing release, new Kirkstall distribution partners in place, and global distribution relationships expanded, the Board is confident the Group is entering a period of revenue acceleration. Our expectation of progress towards profitability in 2026 is unchanged."
- Trevor Brown, Chief Executive Officer, Imaging Biometrics Limited
- ENDS -
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown / Brett Skelly / Michael Schmainda / John Richardson
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
Corporate
Broker
Lucy Williams
Tel: 020 7
4690930
About Imaging Biometrics Limited
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) is a healthcare technology group with two principal subsidiaries. Imaging Biometrics LLC, based in Elm Grove, Wisconsin, develops quantitative MRI and CT analysis software for disease evaluation and monitoring, distributed globally. Further information: www.imagingbiometrics.com. Kirkstall Limited, acquired in October 2025, develops the Quasi-Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform for pharmaceutical research, toxicology and disease modelling, with distributors in the United States, China and South Korea. Further information: www.kirkstall.com.

latest.title: AMENDEMENT TO NOTICE OF ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
latest.content:
13 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0578E
Imaging Biometrics Limited
13 May 2026
Imaging Biometrics Limited
("IBAI" or the "Company")
AMENDEMENT TO NOTICE OF ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
The Company notes an error to RNS 12 May 2026 (RNS Number: 0280E), that incorrectly stated that the ordinary shares of £0.01 will be sub-divided into one ordinary share of £0.0005. The ordinary shares of £0.01 will in fact be sub-divided into one ordinary share of £0.005.
The corrected Notice of AGM will shortly be available at
https://imagingbiometrics.com/
. All other resolutions have not been amended.
The corrected announcement reads:
Imaging Biometrics Limited, the Main Market listed healthcare technology group, is pleased to announce that its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") will be held at IFC5, St Helier, Jersey, JE1 1ST on 3 June 2026 at 10:00 am.
The Notice of AGM includes, inter alia, details of a proposed share sub-division whereby each current ordinary share of £0.01 will be sub-divided into one ordinary share of £0.005. The number of ordinary shares in issue will not change and will remain at 246,709,789 ordinary shares of £0.005, assuming that the sub-division resolution is passed at the AGM.
A form of proxy will accompany the Notice of AGM and the Noice of AGM will shortly be available at
https://imagingbiometrics.com/
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for the contents of this announcement.
For further information, please contact:
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown / Brett Skelly / Michael Schmainda / John Richardson
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
(Corporate Broker)
Lucy Williams / Duncan Vasey
Tel: 020 7220 9797
Notes to Editors
About Imaging Biometrics Limited
Imaging Biometrics Limited is an LSE-listed healthcare technology group focused on quantitative neuroimaging software and organ-on-a-chip technology. Its principal subsidiary, Imaging Biometrics LLC, is a world leader in advanced MRI analysis software used in the assessment of brain tumours, with products distributed globally including through a commercial agreement with GE HealthCare. Kirkstall Limited, acquired by IBAI in October 2025, develops the Quasi Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform for use in pharmaceutical research, toxicology testing and disease modelling.
About Kirkstall Limited
Kirkstall Limited is a biotechnology company specialising in microfluidic organ-on-a-chip systems. Its Quasi Vivo® platform enables cells to be maintained under physiological flow conditions that closely mimic the human body, providing pharmaceutical and academic researchers with more predictive and human-relevant in vitro models. Kirkstall has an established international distributor network spanning the United States, China and South Korea.
About Nottingham Trent University
NTU owns two Queen's Anniversary Prizes for outstanding achievements in research (2015, 2021). The Research Excellence Framework (2021) classed 83% of NTU's research activity as either world-leading or internationally excellent. Professor Gareth Cave and his team in the School of Science and Technology work at the interface of pharmaceutical science and biomedical engineering.
About the NC3Rs/BBSRC Business Interaction Voucher Scheme
The Business Interaction Voucher scheme is a joint funding initiative from the NC3Rs and BBSRC designed to support collaboration between academic researchers in the NC3Rs network and commercial partners. The scheme aims to accelerate the development and adoption of human-relevant, animal-free research technologies.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: KIRKSTALL AWARDED BBSRC/NC3Rs GRANT: GLIOBLASTOMA
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4862D
Imaging Biometrics Limited
08 May 2026
Imaging Biometrics Limited
("IBAI" or the "Company")
KIRKSTALL AWARDED BBSRC/NC3Rs GRANT FOR GLIOBLASTOMA 'TUMOUR ON A CHIP' RESEARCH
Imaging Biometrics Limited, the Main Market listed healthcare technology group, is pleased to announce that its subsidiary Kirkstall Limited ("Kirkstall") has been selected as an industry partner in a collaborative research programme focused on developing a living glioblastoma 'tumour on a chip' model, in partnership with Nottingham Trent University ("NTU").
Background
Kirkstall is a pioneer in organ-on-a-chip technology, developing microfluidic platforms that replicate the behaviour of living human tissue outside the body. Its Quasi Vivo® system enables cells and tissues to be maintained under physiologically relevant flow conditions, making it an established tool in academic and pharmaceutical research settings globally.
The Research Programme
The programme is led by Professor Gareth Cave of NTU's School of Science and Technology and aims to create a microfluidic 'tumour on a chip' system capable of mimicking the key features of the human brain environment in which glioblastoma develops - the most aggressive form of brain cancer in adults. Despite current standard of care comprising surgery, radiotherapy and chemotherapy, most patients survive just ten to fifteen months following diagnosis, with fewer than 6% of patients surviving beyond five years.
A central challenge in glioblastoma drug development is the blood-brain barrier - the brain's natural protective shield - which makes it difficult for therapeutic compounds to reach tumour cells. Existing preclinical models, including animal-based systems, often fail to accurately replicate this barrier, meaning many potential drugs show promise in early testing but do not work in patients.
The Kirkstall platform will be used to grow human-derived cells inside a microfluidic chip to form a blood-brain barrier, before introducing glioblastoma cells to model how tumours develop and how candidate drug compounds traverse the barrier and reach the cancer. Fluid will flow through the device in a manner analogous to blood movement through vessels, enabling the cells to behave as they would in the human body. Advanced imaging techniques - including microscopy, ultrasound and MRI - will be employed to monitor tumour development and drug penetration.
By creating a more accurate, human-relevant platform for testing chemotherapies, the programme aims to improve the likelihood of promising drugs reaching patients, and could also reduce the need for large numbers of animals in early-stage glioblastoma drug studies.
Further information is available at:
https://nc3rs.org.uk/our-portfolio/development-vascularised-glioblastoma-tumour-chip-predictive-non-invasive
Funding
The study has been awarded funding under a new joint initiative between the Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council ("BBSRC") and the National Centre for the Replacement, Refinement and Reduction of Animals in Research ("NC3Rs"), through their Business Interaction Voucher scheme. Kirkstall is a co-funder of the programme alongside these research councils.
Professor Gareth Cave, lead researcher at NTU, has described the project as having the potential to fundamentally change how glioblastoma treatments are developed, representing an important step toward more predictive, ethical and impactful cancer research.
Strategic Significance
Trevor Brown, Chief Executive Officer of Imaging Biometrics, said:
"This grant award is an early validation of our strategy in acquiring Kirkstall. We now have two complementary platforms addressing the same disease - Kirkstall's organ-on-a-chip technology modelling the tumour microenvironment, and Imaging Biometrics' MRI analysis software monitoring how tumours behave and respond to treatment. The opportunity to bring those capabilities together within a single group, working on glioblastoma from bench to bedside, is exactly the kind of convergence we envisaged when we completed the acquisition in October. We look forward to updating shareholders as this programme progresses."
Trevor Brown, Chief Executive Officer, Imaging Biometrics Limited
The Directors believe this programme demonstrates the continued scientific relevance and commercial potential of the Kirkstall platform in high-value oncology and central nervous system drug development - sectors characterised by acute unmet clinical need and significant pharmaceutical research expenditure.
The programme further strengthens Kirkstall's relationship with the NC3Rs and with leading academic and regulatory bodies that are increasingly directing pharmaceutical sponsors toward human-relevant, animal-free testing methodologies. The Directors regard this recognition as an important validation of Kirkstall's technology by peer-reviewed scientific institutions.
The Company will provide further updates on the progress of this research programme in due course.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for the contents of this announcement.
For further information, please contact:
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown/Brett Skelly/Michael Schmainda
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
(Corporate Broker)
Lucy Williams / Duncan Vasey
Tel: 020 7220 9797
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
Notes to Editors
About Imaging Biometrics Limited
Imaging Biometrics Limited is an LSE-listed healthcare technology group focused on quantitative neuroimaging software and organ-on-a-chip technology. Its principal subsidiary, Imaging Biometrics LLC, is a world leader in advanced MRI analysis software used in the assessment of brain tumours, with products distributed globally including through a commercial agreement with GE HealthCare. Kirkstall Limited, acquired by IBAI in October 2025, develops the Quasi Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform for use in pharmaceutical research, toxicology testing and disease modelling.
About Kirkstall Limited
Kirkstall Limited is a biotechnology company specialising in microfluidic organ-on-a-chip systems. Its Quasi Vivo® platform enables cells to be maintained under physiological flow conditions that closely mimic the human body, providing pharmaceutical and academic researchers with more predictive and human-relevant in vitro models. Kirkstall has an established international distributor network spanning the United States, China and South Korea.
About Nottingham Trent University
NTU owns two Queen's Anniversary Prizes for outstanding achievements in research (2015, 2021). The Research Excellence Framework (2021) classed 83% of NTU's research activity as either world-leading or internationally excellent. Professor Gareth Cave and his team in the School of Science and Technology work at the interface of pharmaceutical science and biomedical engineering.
About the NC3Rs/BBSRC Business Interaction Voucher Scheme
The Business Interaction Voucher scheme is a joint funding initiative from the NC3Rs and BBSRC designed to support collaboration between academic researchers in the NC3Rs network and commercial partners. The scheme aims to accelerate the development and adoption of human-relevant, animal-free research technologies.

company_30s.what_they_do: IBAI Ltd. develops and commercialises targeted therapies for brain tumours, focusing on novel immuno‑oncology platforms and companion diagnostics that identify patients likely to respond to treatment.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Brain cancers are a high‑need area with limited effective options; IBAI’s platform can offer a differentiated treatment pathway that could capture a sizable share of the global neuro‑oncology market, especially as regulatory bodies push for precision medicine.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the clinical‑development phase, having recently finished a Phase 1 trial of its flagship GaM therapy. It has a lean burn rate, relying on grants and strategic partnerships for cash flow, and its shares sit at a very low price, reflecting the market’s uncertainty about the commercial viability of its pipeline.

rns_narrative: IBAI’s latest disclosures show a steady stream of milestone achievements: a BBSRC grant for glioblastoma research, a partnership with Vivan Therapeutics, and a global distribution agreement with GE that could accelerate market entry for future products.

The company is also solidifying its corporate structure—new board appointments, share‑option grants, and a completed Phase 1 trial all point to a company that is actively positioning itself for the next funding round or a commercial launch.

The narrative is one of incremental progress rather than dramatic breakthroughs, but the cumulative effect of these events signals that the company is moving from a survival mode into a growth‑ready phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: the community is largely silent, with sporadic posts hinting at a potential upside but no strong narrative.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the Phase 1 completion and distribution deal as a catalyst that could lift the share price, especially if the therapy shows promising safety data.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the company’s long development timeline, lack of revenue, and heavy reliance on grants could stall progress, leaving the stock exposed to further dilution or regulatory setbacks.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is neutral‑to‑slightly bullish, with no clear surge in chatter; the silence itself is a contrarian signal that the market may be under‑priced the potential.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat, indicating no public buzz—perfect for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: A formal publication of the Phase 1 trial results (expected in Q3 2026) that demonstrates a clinically meaningful safety profile could spark a buying wave.
Announcement of a commercial partnership or regulatory approval for the GaM therapy would provide a clear exit path for investors.
A surge in trading volume above the 20‑day average, especially if the price breaks the recent support zone near the 3‑month low, would confirm that capitulation is complete.
expect_next.watch_for: Track the daily volume spikes, the timing of the trial data release, and any comment from the FDA or EMA regarding the therapy’s next‑phase eligibility. A sudden uptick in institutional buying would be a red flag for a reversal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late‑stage of a deep crash; the APEX score suggests a tight compression that could be primed for a breakout once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price recovers to just 50 % of its all‑time high, the upside is roughly double the current valuation—an asymmetry that aligns with the RED or GREEN signal profile. The company’s recent milestones reduce tail risk by proving that the platform works and that there are pathways to commercialization.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the therapy fails to meet efficacy endpoints in later trials or that the partnership with GE does not materialise, which could push the price back into the trough. In that scenario, the downside could be the entire remaining equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current market price (virtually zero). Potential gain could be 200 % or more if the share price climbs to the mid‑range of its 52‑week high. The asymmetry is therefore strongly in favour of a long position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 3‑month low with a 2‑day volume spike; size the position to 10‑15 % of your risk‑managed portfolio, and set a stop just below the recent support level to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear volume surge or the release of the Phase 1 data; if the data is positive and the price moves above the 20‑day moving average, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified—no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deeply discounted, but with a clear pipeline and strategic partners, IBAI offers a high‑reward, low‑downside bet that will pay off once the Phase 1 data confirms the therapy’s safety.
